qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSE-C encryption uses deterministic nonce violating GCM security requirements
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns deterministic AES-GCM nonce derivation in SSE-C at rustfs/src/storage/ecfs.rs, not the advisory's read_file_stream path traversal in crates/ecstore/src/disk/local.rs exposed through /rustfs/rpc/read_file_stream.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Vault KMS backend uses XOR instead of AES-GCM for encryption
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns XOR-based encryption in the Vault KMS backend at crates/kms/src/backends/vault.rs, not the advisory's missing path validation in local disk read_file_stream for /rustfs/rpc/read_file_stream.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying_vuln finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is RustFS path traversal in /rustfs/rpc/read_file_stream caused by joining attacker-controlled paths in crates/ecstore/src/disk/local.rs without canonicalization or boundary checks. The qualifying findings are unrelated cryptographic failures in SSE-C nonce generation and the Vault KMS backend. The only path traversal-themed final finding is admin_rpc_path_traversal, but it is status=non_qualifying_vuln and describes different admin RPC ReadFile/PutFile code.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-pq29-69jg-9mxc describes CWE-22 path traversal in /rustfs/rpc/read_file_stream with root cause volume_dir.join(Path::new(&path)) in crates/ecstore/src/disk/local.rs.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists only ssec_nonce_reuse and vault_xor_encryption as final status=qualifying_vuln findings.
ssec_nonce_reuse/vulnerability_report.json identifies deterministic MD5-derived AES-GCM nonces in rustfs/src/storage/ecfs.rs, a different source path and root cause.
vault_xor_encryption/vulnerability_report.json identifies XOR encryption in crates/kms/src/backends/vault.rs, a different source path and root cause.
admin_rpc_path_traversal/vulnerability_report.json is status=non_qualifying_vuln, so it cannot be selected as the qualifying advisory match.
